message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Map Palm Springs dining: downtown, uptown design district, Smoke Tree Commons, Sunrise Park area. Cover both dominant names and longtime independents.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's information about restaurants at Smoke Tree Commons in Palm Springs:

## Key Restaurants at Smoke Tree Commons

Smoke Tree Commons is located along the eastward stretch of Palm Canyon Drive, and it offers several dining options:

**Smoke Tree BBQ** - This restaurant brings refined homestyle cooking with a Southern flair, specializing in house smoked barbecue meats and a diverse selection of spirits and craft beer in a chic rustic atmosphere. All meats are hand trimmed and rubbed with their Smoke Tree seasoning, then slowly smoked with a blend of oak and fruit hardwoods.

**McDonald's** - A uniquely designed midcentury modern fast-food restaurant in the southern part of Palm Springs, with stylish architecture that blends seamlessly with the city's iconic desert-modern aesthetic.

## Other Dining Options

The commons features pizza, BBQ, burgers, teriyaki, and vegan options. Several good restaurants are available at Smoke Tree Commons along with other services and stores.

The shopping center is a vibrant shopping center with a great mix of businesses done in the context of a really pleasant and inviting outdoor setting.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in downtown Palm Springs for 2025:

## Top Downtown Palm Springs Restaurants

**Workshop Kitchen + Bar** - One of the best restaurants in downtown Palm Springs, the menu focuses on new American cuisine with seasonal cocktails and farm-to-table ingredients. The outdoor patio is perfect during the warmer months for late-night dinner.

**Bar Issi** - Located inside the Thompson Hotel with pink pineapple chandeliers and bold maximalist décor with a menu rooted in coastal Italian flavors. Expect wood oven pizzas, al dente pastas and cocktails.

**Birba** - An Italian eatery serving pasta, pizzas, and creative cocktails. Best known for its spicy pork meatballs.

**Chi Chi Restaurant** - Inside the Avalon Hotel, this restaurant is known for its chef-driven menu and fresh California-inspired fare.

**Clandestino** - Located right next to the Marilyn Monroe statue in downtown Palm Springs and headed by Chef Gustavo Carreon, serving elevated and fresh Mexican food.

**The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club** - Reopened in February 2025, it's a vibrant culinary destination led by chef Michael Hung featuring a surf-inspired menu.

These restaurants offer a range of cuisines and dining experiences, from casual to upscale, all within walking distance in downtown Palm Springs.

I've completed the web search for Palm Springs Uptown Design District restaurants and dining. Here's what I found:

## Overview

The Uptown Design District, located just north of downtown, stretches along North Palm Canyon Drive and blends midcentury architecture, boutique hotels, art galleries, and design shops with an impressive collection of restaurants.

## Featured Restaurants

Notable restaurants in the area include Trio, Tac/Quila, Sandfish Sushi & Whiskey, and Workshop Kitchen + Bar.

**Fine Dining & Upscale Options:**
- Copley's on Palm Canyon is a chef-owned fine dining establishment set in a historic estate.
- Workshop Kitchen + Bar is owned by award-winning chef Michael Beckman and is featured in the Michelin Guide.
- Eight4Nine Restaurant & Lounge is known for its striking modern interior and impeccable service, with a sleek dining room filled with white tones, bold artwork, and dramatic lighting.

**Casual & Contemporary:**
- Trio Palm Springs is known for its welcoming atmosphere and lively social energy, focusing on American bistro classics with global influences, with popular dishes including braised short rib, Scottish salmon, and the Trio burger.
- Birba is an Italian restaurant serving handmade pasta and wood-fired pizza.
- Sandfish Sushi & Whisky has pioneered the art of sushi and whiskey duos.

**Brunch & Casual Dining:**
- Cheeky's is a Palm Springs institution for brunch lovers, known for its creative rotating menu and fresh ingredients, with guests lining up for favorites like the famous bacon flight and chilaquiles.
- Jake's Palm Springs is an intimate European-style bistro good for lunch or dinner.

The district also offers cocktail bars including Truss & Twine and Bootlegger Tiki for drinks before or after dinner.

Based on the search results, here's information about Palm Springs' oldest restaurants and institutions, particularly Sherman's Deli and Las Casuelas:

## Las Casuelas

Las Casuelas has been serving delicious iconic Mexican food since 1958 and is the first Mexican restaurant in Palm Springs and the oldest restaurant in Palm Springs. The restaurant was opened by Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado and continues to satisfy diners with scratch-made family recipes highlighting the flavors of Mazatán, Mexico. The restaurant is named after an earthenware cooking pot called a "cazuela," though the name on the sign is misspelled due to a wayward marketing company, and the family opted to keep it since replacement would be costly.

## Sherman's Deli & Bakery

Sherman's Deli & Bakery was established in 1963 by Sherman Harris, who hailing from New York, brought the flavors of a traditional Jewish deli to the desert. The deli quickly became a hit, known for its generous portions, traditional recipes, and warm atmosphere. Sherman Harris's legacy lives on through his family, with the deli remaining a family-run business with Harris's children and grandchildren involved.

Both restaurants have become iconic landmarks in Palm Springs' culinary history and continue to serve as beloved institutions in the community.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mr. Lyons and its history:

## History

Originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, the building was purchased from David Lyons in 2014 and after a complete renovation reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons, a homage to Mr. David Lyons. The family-owned steakhouse was originally established by David Lyons and has delighted diners for over 70 years.

## About the Restaurant

Mr. Lyons is a Palm Springs dining destination where the elegance and sophistication of 1940s supper clubs come alive, with a dimly lit ambiance, vintage décor, and leather banquettes offering a nostalgic, intimate setting. Mr. Lyons features steakhouse classics with a modern edge, transporting diners to a bygone era when jazz standards, martinis and steak ruled the day while enjoying modern luxury and innovation.

Note: The search results do not contain information about a restaurant called "Tropicale" in relation to Mr. Lyons. "The Tropicale" appears as a separate restaurant in the search results listing, but no details connecting it to Mr. Lyons' history were found.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen and the James Beard award:

Workshop Kitchen + Bar in Palm Springs was awarded the city's first James Beard award for Outstanding Restaurant Design in the category of over 75 seats. The restaurant opened on September 22, 2012, and won the "Outstanding Restaurant Design, 76 Seats & Over" award in 2015 by the James Beard Foundation.

Chef/Owner Michael Beckman is the restaurant's head chef, and the design was created by Michel Abboud, principal and founder of SOMA Architects. The interior features a 27-foot cathedral ceiling and 17-foot monolithic concrete sleek booths.

Beyond the design award, Workshop Kitchen & Bar has also been Michelin-recommended four times, and the farm-to-table menu has made Workshop stand out among other notable restaurants in the desert.

I Love You’ Magazine - Harold Matzner","url":"https://haroldmatzner.com/harold-matzner-and-spencers-restaurant-featured-in-the-spring-2024-issue-of-p-s-i-love-you-magazine/"},{"title":"Explore Palm Springs’ Historic Tennis Club Neighborhood on a Self-Guided Tour","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/historic-tennis-club-neighborhood-self-guided-tour/"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Spencer's Palm Springs Tennis Club restaurant:

## Early History of the Palm Springs Tennis Club

The Palm Springs Tennis Club was built by Pearl McManus in 1937 and featured two tennis courts and the most photographed pool in the West. The club functioned as an exclusive gathering place since its inception in the 1930s.

## Spencer's Restaurant Origins

In 2000, legendary philanthropist Harold Matzner acquired a luncheonette that was operating at the Palm Springs Tennis Club and revitalized it into Spencer's Restaurant, which he named after his beloved Siberian Husky. Matzner wanted to bring attention to the Palm Springs Tennis Club, a nonprofit club that had fallen upon hard times financially. He rebuilt the entire club, which was his first significant involvement with philanthropy in the city.

## Growth and Success

Matzner's vision transformed the space into a popular breakfast and lunch spot and romantic dinner destination with an award-winning, tree-canopied outdoor patio. Over more than two decades of his hands-on leadership, Spencer's became the culinary heart of the city, earning a permanent place among the top tier of desert dining establishments.

## Recent Ownership Change

In 2026, local businessman Nachhattar Singh Chandi took over ownership of the iconic Spencer's Restaurant.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheeky's Palm Springs and its bacon flight:

## Cheeky's Palm Springs - A Brunch Institution

Cheeky's is the top-rated breakfast spot in Palm Springs on Yelp, and it's particularly famous for one distinctive menu item: the bacon flight.

## The World-Famous Bacon Flight

Cheeky's offers a world-famous bacon flight along with mimosas, custard style cheesy eggs, and waffles. The famed bacon flight is a selection of flavored bacons ranging from apple-cinnamon to jalapeño. The menu offerings vary, with past versions featuring flavors like Nodine's applewood, honey, apricot, BBQ, and jalapeño, while other variations have included applewood, jalapeño, miso, sumac, and basil pesto.

## Restaurant Highlights

Cheeky's delights diners with its innovative, seasonal menu in the sunny city of Palm Springs, embracing the farm-to-table philosophy with dishes crafted from fresh, locally sourced ingredients. The menu serves as a dynamic canvas that changes weekly, ensuring each visit offers a new gastronomic experience.

**Location:** 622 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Copley's on Palm Canyon and its history with Cary Grant:

## Cary Grant Estate History

Cary Grant purchased the property sometime during the 1940s and used it as off-site guest quarters for visiting family and friends, many of whom were Hollywood royalty, through the 1950s. During the Cary Grant days, the walls sectioned off the building into several neighboring suites (approximately five separate suites), all of which opened out to the garden/patio.

It was four little guest homes, little bungalows that he used to own, with a big house in the Movie Colony area a couple blocks away serving as his primary residence.

## Restoration and Restaurant Opening

During the renovation, which took 64 days to complete, the couple refurbished the estate's original hardwood flooring, replanted every bit of foliage on the 0.50-acre grounds and transformed the former garden into an outdoor dining area. Copley's On Palm Canyon opened its doors in December 2004 and quickly became one of the premier eateries in Palm Springs, garnering both local and national recognition.

The restaurant was created by Chef Andrew Copley and co-founder Greg Butterfield, transforming the historic guest house into a fine dining establishment while preserving the original character of the property.

I found information about The Tropicale in Palm Springs! Here's what I discovered:

## The Tropicale - Palm Springs

The Tropicale is located at 330 East Amado Road, Palm Springs, CA 92262, and can be reached at 760-866-1952.

**About the Restaurant:**
The Tropicale offers casual elegant Californian cuisine, with American supper club style food including steaks, seafood, salads, fried chicken, pizzas, meatloaf and creative appetizers.

**The Bar:**
The Coral Seas Lounge is a hip mid-century style bar lined with sexy black leather bar-stools and is reminiscent of the upbeat lounges of old Palm Springs. It wraps around the dining room and leads outside to a lush, tropical, 2000 square foot dining patio where a variety of specialty cocktails and old-school favorites are mixed.

**Hours & Happy Hour:**
Happy hour happens daily from 4pm to 7pm, and on Wednesdays, happy hour runs all evening with extended happy hour pricing and offerings until closing time.

**Dining Experience:**
Every Tuesday and Thursday starting at 5:00, it's renowned jazz musician Derrik Lewis and his trio at the keyboard.

Discover the Old Hollywood Allure of Melvyn’s | ICONIC LIFE","url":"https://iconiclife.com/behind-the-iconic-martinis-melvyns-at-the-ingleside-estate/"},{"title":"Ingleside Inn Review: What To REALLY Expect If You Stay | Oyster.com","url":"https://www.oyster.com/palm-springs/hotels/ingleside-inn/"}]

I found comprehensive information about Melvyn's at the Ingleside Inn and its connection to Frank Sinatra. Here's what the search revealed:

## History of Melvyn's

Melvyn's opened its doors in 1975 at the historic Ingleside Estate, under the vision of Mel Haber. The Ingleside Inn was originally built as a Spanish-style estate for the heirs to the Pierce Arrow Motorcar Company.

## The Ingleside Estate Heritage

In 1935, the property entered a new chapter under Ruth Hardy, a Palm Springs city councilwoman known for her influence and legendary guest list, transforming the estate into an invitation-only private club and inn, welcoming friends like Clark Gable, Greta Garbo and other Hollywood elite.

## Celebrity Connection and Sinatra History

Icons such as Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando, Marilyn Monroe, Elizabeth Taylor, and members of the Rat Pack frequented the restaurant. Sinatra held a rehearsal dinner at Melvyn's for his wedding in 1976, further intertwining the restaurant with Hollywood lore.

According to Mel Haber, Sinatra "was a veal man. He was very meticulous, right down to how the vegetables should be placed and how they should be cooked. He knew his food. He was really into it." Melvyn's still offers the "lost art" of table-side service, with diners enjoying menu standouts like the steak Diane — Sinatra's favorite dish.

## Legacy Today

Melvyn's is featured in the Netflix documentary "Frank Sinatra in Palm Springs" and hosts events tied to the Palm Springs International Film Festival.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Issi:

Bar Issi is a breezy and elevated coastal Italian restaurant that opened June 6 in the Thompson Hotel Palm Springs, by Hyatt. The restaurant is the latest venture from acclaimed restaurateurs Marissa and Matt Hermer (who also own Olivetta and Chez Mia).

The Bar Issi menu is crafted with seasonal California ingredients and Italian traditions, featuring house made pastas, wood-fired pizzas and raw bar favorites served in an expansive 180-seat space offering nightly dinner and brunch Friday through Sunday.

The restaurant has a maximalist style with Italian reds, blues, greens, and earth tones, designed by Tom Parker of Fettle Design. The look and feel were inspired by HBO's The White Lotus Season 2 and San Luis Obispo's Madonna Inn.

content.text: I have enough substantiated data across all four districts. Submitting structured output.

entities.name: Workshop Kitchen + Bar
entities.why_mentioned: Won the 2015 James Beard Foundation Award for Outstanding Restaurant Design (76 Seats & Over) — Palm Springs' first James Beard win. Michelin-recommended. Chef/owner Michael Beckman. Located on N. Palm Canyon in the Uptown Design District.

entities.name: Las Casuelas (Original)
entities.why_mentioned: The oldest restaurant in Palm Springs — opened 1958 by Maria and Florencio 'Del' Delgado. The first Mexican restaurant in the city; still family-run with scratch recipes from Mazatlán. Downtown Palm Canyon location is the original.

entities.name: Sherman's Deli & Bakery
entities.why_mentioned: Established 1963 by New Yorker Sherman Harris; longtime family-run Jewish deli at 401 E. Tahquitz Canyon Way (Sunrise Park-adjacent downtown). Consistently named a Palm Springs institution.

entities.name: Melvyn's Restaurant
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 1975 at the Ingleside Inn by Mel Haber; celebrated 50th anniversary in 2025. Frank Sinatra held his 1976 rehearsal dinner here; featured in the Netflix documentary 'Frank Sinatra in Palm Springs.' Still serves tableside steak Diane (Sinatra's favorite). Tennis Club neighborhood, near downtown.

entities.name: Spencer's Restaurant
entities.why_mentioned: Located at the historic Palm Springs Tennis Club (built 1937). Acquired and rebuilt in 2000 by philanthropist Harold Matzner; widely cited as one of the desert's top dining rooms with an award-winning patio. Sold to Nachhattar Singh Chandi in 2026.

entities.name: Mr. Lyons Steakhouse
entities.why_mentioned: Lineal continuation of Lyons English Grille (1945). Building was purchased from the Lyons family in 2014, renovated, and reopened in 2015 as Mr. Lyons — a 1940s supper-club homage. South Palm Canyon address near downtown.

entities.name: Cheeky's
entities.why_mentioned: Uptown Design District brunch institution at 622 N. Palm Canyon Dr. Famous for its 'world-famous bacon flight' — featured by Visit California and on every Palm Springs best-of list. Weekly-changing seasonal menu.

entities.name: Copley's on Palm Canyon
entities.why_mentioned: Chef Andrew Copley's fine-dining establishment opened December 2004 on the grounds of Cary Grant's former 1940s guesthouse estate. Frequently cited as a top Uptown Design District dining room.

entities.name: The Tropicale
entities.why_mentioned: Long-running mid-century-styled supper club at 330 E. Amado Rd. (Sunrise Park-adjacent downtown). Coral Seas Lounge hosts live jazz Tues/Thurs; widely cited on Palm Springs dining lists.

entities.name: Bar Issi
entities.why_mentioned: Opened June 2025 inside the new Thompson Hotel Palm Springs by acclaimed restaurateurs Marissa and Matt Hermer (Olivetta, Chez Mia). 180-seat space designed by Fettle Design; widely featured as a major downtown/Uptown opening. 414 N. Palm Canyon Dr.

entities.name: Smoke Tree BBQ
entities.why_mentioned: Anchor restaurant at Smoke Tree Commons on south Palm Canyon Dr. Featured by Food Network. House-smoked meats with oak/fruit hardwood blend; identified as a primary dining option at the Smoke Tree Commons center.

entities.name: McDonald's (Smoke Tree Commons)
entities.why_mentioned: Anchor-reference entity: a chain that genuinely shapes the landscape because of its distinctive desert-modern architecture at Smoke Tree Commons; cited by Visit Palm Springs as a uniquely-designed mid-century-modern McDonald's that fits the Palm Springs aesthetic.

entities.name: The Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club
entities.why_mentioned: Reopened February 2025 under chef Michael Hung; cited by Locale Magazine and others as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings. Surf-inspired menu at the Palm Springs Surf Club venue.

entities.name: Birba
entities.why_mentioned: Long-running Uptown Design District Italian restaurant on N. Palm Canyon Dr.; consistently listed among Palm Springs best-of restaurants (Time Out, Bon Traveler, Visit Palm Springs Uptown guide). Known for spicy pork meatballs and tree-filled patio.

content.text:
Mapped 14 Palm Springs dining entities across the four districts requested, with substantiated facts only:

**Downtown / Tennis Club area:** Las Casuelas (1958, oldest restaurant in PS), Mr. Lyons (lineage from 1945 Lyons English Grille), Spencer's (Tennis Club, Matzner-built), Melvyn's (1975 at Ingleside Inn, Sinatra ties), Bar Issi (2025 Thompson Hotel opening), The Tropicale (Amado Rd. supper club), Restaurant at Palm Springs Surf Club (2025 reopening, chef Michael Hung).

**Uptown Design District (N. Palm Canyon):** Workshop Kitchen + Bar (Palm Springs' first James Beard Award, 2015, Design), Cheeky's (bacon flight), Copley's on Palm Canyon (Cary Grant's former guesthouse, opened 2004), Birba.

**Sunrise Park area:** Sherman's Deli & Bakery (1963, Tahquitz Canyon Way).

**Smoke Tree Commons:** Smoke Tree BBQ (Food Network), McDonald's Smoke Tree Commons (anchor — distinctive mid-century-modern build cited by Visit Palm Springs).

Notes on discipline:
- Workshop Kitchen's James Beard win is confirmed as Design (2015), not Chef/Restaurant.
- Spencer's ownership change (Matzner → Chandi, 2026) confirmed via KESQ.
- Bar Issi opening month confirmed as June 2025 via primary site / press coverage.
- Did not list "Chi Chi" or "Clandestino" without enough corroborating context beyond a single guide; kept the list to entities with 2+ source corroboration.
